question: What is the maximum mpg of the cars that had 8 cylinders or that were produced before 1980 ?
basic_sql: SELECT MAX(T1.MPG) AS MaxMPG FROM cars_data AS T1 WHERE T1.Cylinders = 8 OR T1.Year < 1980

basic_sql: SELECT c.course_id, c.course_name FROM Courses AS c JOIN Sections AS s ON c.course_id = s.course_id GROUP BY c.course_id, c.course_name HAVING COUNT(s.section_id) <= 2

question: Find the kind of program which most number of students are enrolled in?
basic_sql: WITH EnrolledDegrees AS (SELECT dp.degree_summary_name, COUNT(se.student_enrolment_id) AS enrollment_count FROM Degree_Programs AS dp JOIN Student_Enrolment AS se ON dp.degree_program_id = se.degree_program_id GROUP BY dp.degree_summary_name) SELECT degree_summary_name, enrollment_count FROM EnrolledDegrees ORDER BY enrollment_count DESC LIMIT 1

question: Among the cars that do not have the minimum horsepower , what are the make ids and names of all those with less than 4 cylinders ?
basic_sql: SELECT T1.MakeId, T2.Make FROM cars_data AS T1 JOIN car_names AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.MakeId WHERE T1.Horsepower <> (SELECT MIN(Horsepower) FROM cars_data) AND T1.Cylinders < 4

question: Find the last name of the students who currently live in the state of North Carolina but have not registered in any degree program.
basic_sql: SELECT DISTINCT s.last_name FROM Students AS s JOIN Addresses AS a ON s.current_address_id = a.address_id WHERE a.state_province_county = 'North Carolina' AND NOT s.student_id IN (SELECT se.student_id FROM Student_Enrolment AS se)

question: What is the model for the car with a weight smaller than the average?
basic_sql: WITH avg_weight AS (SELECT AVG(Weight) AS avg_weight FROM cars_data) SELECT T1.Model FROM model_list AS T1 JOIN cars_data AS T2 ON T1.ModelId = T2.ModelId WHERE T2.Weight < (SELECT avg_weight FROM avg_weight)
robust_sql: WITH avg_weight AS (SELECT AVG(CAST(REPLACE(TRIM(T2.Weight, ' '), ',', '') AS REAL)) AS avg_weight FROM cars_data AS T2), cleaned_cars_data AS (SELECT CASE WHEN REPLACE(TRIM(T2.Weight, ' '), ',', '') IS NULL THEN (SELECT avg_weight FROM avg_weight) ELSE CAST(REPLACE(TRIM(T2.Weight, ' '), ',', '') AS REAL) END AS Weight, T2.ModelId FROM cars_data AS T2), deduplicated_model_list AS (SELECT DISTINCT ModelId, Model FROM model_list) SELECT T1.Model FROM deduplicated_model_list AS T1 JOIN cleaned_cars_data AS T2 ON T1.ModelId = T2.ModelId WHERE T2.Weight < (SELECT avg_weight FROM avg_weight)

question: How many countries has more than 2 car makers ?
basic_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.CountryId) FROM countries AS T1 JOIN car_makers AS T2 ON T1.CountryId = T2.Country GROUP BY T1.CountryId HAVING COUNT(T2.Maker) > 2

question: What is the number of car models that are produced by each maker and what is the id and full name of each maker?

question: What is the count of the car models produced in the United States?

question: What is the name and id of the department with the most number of degrees ?
basic_sql: SELECT d.department_name, d.department_id FROM Departments AS d JOIN Degree_Programs AS dp ON d.department_id = dp.department_id GROUP BY d.department_name, d.department_id ORDER BY COUNT(dp.degree_program_id) DESC LIMIT 1

question: Find the semester when both Master students and Bachelor students got enrolled in.
basic_sql: WITH master_students AS (SELECT DISTINCT semester_id FROM Student_Enrolment JOIN Degree_Programs ON Student_Enrolment.degree_program_id = Degree_Programs.degree_program_id WHERE degree_summary_name = 'Master'), bachelor_students AS (SELECT DISTINCT semester_id FROM Student_Enrolment JOIN Degree_Programs ON Student_Enrolment.degree_program_id = Degree_Programs.degree_program_id WHERE degree_summary_name = 'Bachelor') SELECT semester_id FROM master_students INTERSECT SELECT semester_id FROM bachelor_students
robust_sql: WITH master_students AS (SELECT DISTINCT semester_id FROM Student_Enrolment JOIN Degree_Programs ON Student_Enrolment.degree_program_id = Degree_Programs.degree_program_id WHERE degree_summary_name = 'Master'), bachelor_students AS (SELECT DISTINCT semester_id FROM Student_Enrolment JOIN Degree_Programs ON Student_Enrolment.degree_program_id = Degree_Programs.degree_program_id WHERE degree_summary_name = 'Bachelor') SELECT semester_id FROM master_students INTERSECT SELECT semester_id FROM bachelor_students

question: What are the last name of the students who live in North Carolina but have not registered in any degree programs?
basic_sql: SELECT DISTINCT s.last_name FROM Students AS s JOIN Addresses AS a ON s.current_address_id = a.address_id LEFT JOIN Student_Enrolment AS se ON s.student_id = se.student_id WHERE a.state_province_county = 'North Carolina' AND se.student_id IS NULL
robust_sql: WITH Cleaned_Addresses AS (SELECT address_id, line_1, line_2, line_3, city, zip_postcode, state_province_county, country, other_address_details, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY address_id ORDER BY address_id) AS rn FROM Addresses), Cleaned_Students AS (SELECT student_id, current_address_id, permanent_address_id, first_name, middle_name, last_name, cell_mobile_number, email_address, ssn, date_first_registered, date_left, other_student_details, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY student_id ORDER BY student_id) AS rn FROM Students), Cleaned_Degree_Programs AS (SELECT degree_program_id, department_id, degree_summary_name, degree_summary_description, other_details, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY degree_program_id ORDER BY degree_program_id) AS rn FROM Degree_Programs), Cleaned_Student_Enrolment AS (SELECT student_enrolment_id, degree_program_id, semester_id, student_id, other_details, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY student_enrolment_id ORDER BY student_enrolment_id) AS rn FROM Student_Enrolment) SELECT DISTINCT cs.last_name FROM Cleaned_Students AS cs JOIN Cleaned_Addresses AS ca ON cs.current_address_id = ca.address_id LEFT JOIN Cleaned_Student_Enrolment AS cse ON cs.student_id = cse.student_id WHERE ca.state_province_county = 'North Carolina' AND cse.student_id IS NULL

question: What is the id of the semester that had both Masters and Bachelors students enrolled?
basic_sql: WITH masters_students AS (SELECT DISTINCT semester_id FROM Student_Enrolment JOIN Degrees ON Student_Enrolment.degree_program_id = Degrees.degree_program_id WHERE Degrees.degree_summary_name = 'Masters'), bachelors_students AS (SELECT DISTINCT semester_id FROM Student_Enrolment JOIN Degrees ON Student_Enrolment.degree_program_id = Degrees.degree_program_id WHERE Degrees.degree_summary_name = 'Bachelor''s') SELECT semester_id FROM masters_students INTERSECT SELECT semester_id FROM bachelors_students
robust_sql: WITH masters_students AS (SELECT DISTINCT semester_id FROM Student_Enrolment JOIN Degrees ON Student_Enrolment.degree_program_id = Degrees.degree_program_id WHERE Degrees.degree_summary_name = 'Masters'), bachelors_students AS (SELECT DISTINCT semester_id FROM Student_Enrolment JOIN Degrees ON Student_Enrolment.degree_program_id = Degrees.degree_program_id WHERE Degrees.degree_summary_name = 'Bachelor''s') SELECT semester_id FROM masters_students INTERSECT SELECT semester_id FROM bachelors_students

question: What is the degree summary name that has the most number of students enrolled?
basic_sql: SELECT dp.degree_summary_name, COUNT(se.student_id) AS student_count FROM Degree_Programs AS dp JOIN Student_Enrolment AS se ON dp.degree_program_id = se.degree_program_id GROUP BY dp.degree_summary_name ORDER BY student_count DESC LIMIT 1

basic_sql: WITH makers AS (SELECT DISTINCT Id, Maker, FullName FROM car_makers) SELECT m.Maker, m.FullName, COUNT(DISTINCT ml.Model) AS ModelCount FROM makers AS m JOIN model_list AS ml ON m.Id = ml.Maker GROUP BY m.Maker, m.FullName
robust_sql: WITH makers AS (SELECT DISTINCT Id, Maker, FullName FROM car_makers), models AS (SELECT DISTINCT Maker, Model FROM model_list) SELECT m.Maker, m.FullName, COUNT(DISTINCT mo.Model) AS ModelCount FROM makers AS m LEFT JOIN models AS mo ON m.Id = mo.Maker GROUP BY m.Maker, m.FullName

question: Which department offers the most number of degrees? List department name and id.
basic_sql: WITH Deduped_Departments AS (SELECT DISTINCT department_id, department_name FROM Departments), Deduped_Degree_Programs AS (SELECT DISTINCT degree_program_id, department_id FROM Degree_Programs) SELECT dd.department_name, dd.department_id FROM Deduped_Departments AS dd JOIN Deduped_Degree_Programs AS dp ON dd.department_id = dp.department_id GROUP BY dd.department_name, dd.department_id ORDER BY COUNT(dp.degree_program_id) DESC LIMIT 1
robust_sql: WITH Deduped_Departments AS (SELECT DISTINCT department_id, department_name FROM Departments), Deduped_Degree_Programs AS (SELECT DISTINCT degree_program_id, department_id FROM Degree_Programs) SELECT dd.department_name, dd.department_id FROM Deduped_Departments AS dd JOIN Deduped_Degree_Programs AS dp ON dd.department_id = dp.department_id GROUP BY dd.department_name, dd.department_id ORDER BY COUNT(dp.degree_program_id) DESC LIMIT 1

basic_sql: SELECT c.course_id, c.course_name FROM Sections AS s JOIN Courses AS c ON s.course_id = c.course_id GROUP BY c.course_id, c.course_name HAVING COUNT(s.section_id) < 2

question: Which are the car makers which produce at least 2 models and more than 3 car makers ? List the id and the maker .
basic_sql: SELECT T1.Id, T1.Maker FROM car_makers AS T1 JOIN model_list AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.Maker GROUP BY T1.Id, T1.Maker HAVING COUNT(T2.ModelId) >= 2

question: What are the id and names of the countries which have more than 3 car makers or produce the 'fiat' model?
basic_sql: WITH country_makers AS (SELECT c.CountryId, COUNT(cm.Id) AS MakerCount FROM countries AS c JOIN car_makers AS cm ON c.Continent = cm.Country GROUP BY c.CountryId), country_fiat_models AS (SELECT c.CountryId FROM countries AS c JOIN car_makers AS cm ON c.Continent = cm.Country JOIN model_list AS ml ON cm.Id = ml.Maker WHERE ml.Model = 'fiat') SELECT DISTINCT c.CountryId, c.CountryName FROM countries AS c WHERE c.CountryId IN (SELECT CountryId FROM country_makers WHERE MakerCount > 3) OR c.CountryId IN (SELECT CountryId FROM country_fiat_models)

question: What's the name of the course with most number of enrollments?
basic_sql: SELECT c.course_name, COUNT(sec.student_course_id) AS enrollment_count FROM Courses AS c JOIN Student_Enrolment_Courses AS sec ON c.course_id = sec.course_id GROUP BY c.course_name ORDER BY enrollment_count DESC LIMIT 1
robust_sql: WITH Cleaned_Student_Enrolment_Courses AS (SELECT DISTINCT student_course_id FROM Student_Enrolment_Courses) SELECT c.course_name, COUNT(sec.student_course_id) AS enrollment_count FROM Courses AS c JOIN Cleaned_Student_Enrolment_Courses AS sec ON c.course_id = sec.course_id GROUP BY c.course_name ORDER BY enrollment_count DESC LIMIT 1

question: What are the ids and makers of all car makers that produce at least 2 models and make more than 3 cars?
basic_sql: SELECT T1.Id, T1.Maker FROM car_makers AS T1 JOIN model_list AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.Maker GROUP BY T1.Maker HAVING COUNT(T2.Model) >= 2 AND SUM(CASE WHEN NOT T1.Country IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 3

question: What are the ids and names of all countries that either have more than 3 car makers or produce fiat model ?
basic_sql: WITH country_car_makers AS (SELECT c.CountryId, COUNT(cm.Id) AS MakerCount FROM countries AS c JOIN car_makers AS cm ON c.CountryId = cm.Country GROUP BY c.CountryId), country_fiat_models AS (SELECT DISTINCT c.CountryId FROM countries AS c JOIN car_makers AS cm ON c.CountryId = cm.Country JOIN model_list AS ml ON cm.Id = ml.Maker WHERE ml.Model = 'Fiat') SELECT cc.CountryId, cn.CountryName FROM country_car_makers AS cc JOIN countries AS cn ON cc.CountryId = cn.CountryId WHERE cc.MakerCount > 3 UNION SELECT cf.CountryId, cn.CountryName FROM country_fiat_models AS cf JOIN countries AS cn ON cf.CountryId = cn.CountryId

question: What is the name of the country with the most car makers?
basic_sql: WITH country_counts AS (SELECT c.CountryName, COUNT(cm.Id) AS MakerCount FROM countries AS c JOIN car_makers AS cm ON c.CountryId = cm.Country GROUP BY c.CountryName) SELECT CountryName, MakerCount FROM country_counts ORDER BY MakerCount DESC LIMIT 1
robust_sql: WITH country_counts AS (SELECT c.CountryName, COUNT(cm.Id) AS MakerCount FROM (SELECT DISTINCT CountryId FROM countries) AS c JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT Id FROM car_makers WHERE NOT Country IS NULL) AS cm ON c.CountryId = cm.Country GROUP BY c.CountryName), country_stats AS (SELECT Country, COUNT(*) AS TotalCount, SUM(CASE WHEN Country IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS NullCount, SUM(CASE WHEN Country IN (SELECT Country FROM car_makers GROUP BY Country HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT Id) > 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DuplicateCount FROM car_makers GROUP BY Country) SELECT cc.CountryName, cc.MakerCount FROM country_counts AS cc WHERE cc.CountryName IN (SELECT CASE WHEN cs.NullCount > 0 THEN 'Unknown' WHEN cs.DuplicateCount > 0 THEN 'Duplicate' ELSE cs.Country END AS Country FROM country_stats AS cs) ORDER BY MakerCount DESC LIMIT 1

question: Find the model of the car whose weight is below the average weight.
basic_sql: WITH avg_weight AS (SELECT AVG(Weight) AS avg_weight FROM cars_data) SELECT T1.Model FROM model_list AS T1 JOIN cars_data AS T2 ON T1.MakeId = T2.MakeId WHERE T2.Weight < (SELECT avg_weight FROM avg_weight)
robust_sql: WITH avg_weight AS (SELECT AVG(CAST(REPLACE(TRIM(T2.Weight, ' '), ',', '') AS REAL)) AS avg_weight FROM cars_data AS T2), clean_cars_data AS (SELECT Id, CAST(REPLACE(TRIM(Weight, ' '), ',', '') AS REAL) AS Weight FROM cars_data) SELECT T1.Model FROM model_list AS T1 JOIN clean_cars_data AS T2 ON T1.MakeId = T2.MakeId WHERE T2.Weight < (SELECT avg_weight FROM avg_weight)

question: What are the names of all European countries with at least 3 manufacturers?
basic_sql: WITH filtered_countries AS (SELECT DISTINCT CountryId, CountryName FROM countries WHERE Continent = 'Europe'), manufacturer_counts AS (SELECT CountryId, COUNT(Maker) AS ManufacturerCount FROM car_makers GROUP BY CountryId) SELECT fc.CountryName FROM filtered_countries AS fc JOIN manufacturer_counts AS mc ON fc.CountryId = mc.CountryId WHERE mc.ManufacturerCount >= 3

question: Who is enrolled in a Bachelor degree program? List the first name, middle name, last name.
basic_sql: SELECT s.first_name, s.middle_name, s.last_name FROM Students AS s JOIN Student_Enrolment AS se ON s.student_id = se.student_id JOIN Degree_Programs AS dp ON se.degree_program_id = dp.degree_program_id WHERE dp.degree_summary_name = 'Bachelor'

question: What is the maker of the carr produced in the earliest year and what year was it?
basic_sql: SELECT T3.Maker, MIN(T2.Year) AS Earliest_Year FROM cars_data AS T2 JOIN model_list AS T1 ON T1.ModelId = T2.Model JOIN car_makers AS T3 ON T3.Id = T1.Maker GROUP BY T3.Maker

question: Which address holds the most number of students currently? List the address id and all lines.
basic_sql: SELECT a.address_id, a.line_1, a.line_2, a.line_3, COUNT(s.student_id) AS student_count FROM Addresses AS a JOIN Students AS s ON a.address_id = s.current_address_id JOIN Student_Enrolment AS se ON s.student_id = se.student_id WHERE s.date_left IS NULL GROUP BY a.address_id, a.line_1, a.line_2, a.line_3 ORDER BY student_count DESC LIMIT 1
robust_sql: WITH Cleaned_Addresses AS (SELECT DISTINCT address_id, line_1, line_2, line_3, city, zip_postcode, state_province_county, country, other_address_details FROM Addresses), Cleaned_Students AS (SELECT student_id, current_address_id FROM Students WHERE date_left IS NULL), Cleaned_Student_Enrolment AS (SELECT student_id, degree_program_id, semester_id FROM Student_Enrolment) SELECT ca.address_id, ca.line_1, ca.line_2, ca.line_3, COUNT(cs.student_id) AS student_count FROM Cleaned_Addresses AS ca JOIN Cleaned_Students AS cs ON ca.address_id = cs.current_address_id JOIN Cleaned_Student_Enrolment AS cse ON cs.student_id = cse.student_id GROUP BY ca.address_id, ca.line_1, ca.line_2, ca.line_3 ORDER BY student_count DESC LIMIT 1

question: Among the cars with more than lowest horsepower, which ones do not have more than 3 cylinders? List the car makeid and make name.
basic_sql: WITH min_horsepower AS (SELECT MIN(CAST(REPLACE(Horsepower, ',', '') AS REAL)) AS min_hp FROM cars_data WHERE NOT Horsepower IS NULL) SELECT DISTINCT cn.MakeId, cn.Make FROM car_names AS cn JOIN cars_data AS cd ON cn.MakeId = cd.Id WHERE CAST(REPLACE(cd.Horsepower, ',', '') AS REAL) > (SELECT min_hp FROM min_horsepower) AND cd.Cylinders <= 3
robust_sql: WITH min_horsepower AS (SELECT MIN(CAST(REPLACE(Horsepower, ',', '') AS REAL)) AS min_hp FROM cars_data WHERE NOT Horsepower IS NULL), filtered_cars AS (SELECT Id, CAST(REPLACE(Horsepower, ',', '') AS REAL) AS hp, Cylinders FROM cars_data WHERE CAST(REPLACE(Horsepower, ',', '') AS REAL) > (SELECT min_hp FROM min_horsepower) AND Cylinders <= 3) SELECT DISTINCT cn.MakeId, cn.Make FROM car_names AS cn JOIN filtered_cars AS fc ON cn.MakeId = fc.Id

question: Find the program which most number of students are enrolled in. List both the id and the summary.
basic_sql: SELECT dp.degree_program_id, dp.degree_summary_name FROM Degree_Programs AS dp JOIN Student_Enrolment AS se ON dp.degree_program_id = se.degree_program_id GROUP BY dp.degree_program_id, dp.degree_summary_name ORDER BY COUNT(se.student_id) DESC LIMIT 1

question: Find the make and production time of the cars that were produced in the earliest year?
basic_sql: SELECT T1.Make, T2.Year FROM car_names AS T1 JOIN cars_data AS T2 ON T1.Model = T2.Model WHERE NOT T2.Year IS NULL ORDER BY T2.Year ASC LIMIT 1
robust_sql: WITH cleaned_countries AS (SELECT DISTINCT CountryId, CountryName FROM countries), cleaned_car_makers AS (SELECT DISTINCT Id, Maker, FullName, Country FROM car_makers) SELECT T1.Make, T2.Year FROM car_names AS T1 JOIN cars_data AS T2 ON T1.Model = T2.Model JOIN cleaned_countries AS T3 ON T3.CountryId = T1.Country JOIN cleaned_car_makers AS T4 ON T4.Id = T1.MakeId WHERE NOT T2.Year IS NULL ORDER BY T2.Year ASC LIMIT 1

question: How many cars have a larger accelerate than the car with the largest horsepower?
basic_sql: WITH max_horsepower AS (SELECT MAX(Horsepower) AS MaxHorsepower FROM cars_data) SELECT COUNT(*) AS CarCount FROM cars_data WHERE CAST(REPLACE(accelerate, ',', '') AS REAL) > (SELECT MaxHorsepower FROM max_horsepower)
robust_sql: WITH max_horsepower AS (SELECT MAX(CAST(REPLACE(Horsepower, ',', '') AS REAL)) AS MaxHorsepower FROM cars_data), cleaned_cars_data AS (SELECT Id, CAST(REPLACE(accelerate, ',', '') AS REAL) AS Accelerate, CAST(REPLACE(Horsepower, ',', '') AS REAL) AS Horsepower FROM cars_data WHERE NOT Horsepower IS NULL AND NOT Accelerate IS NULL) SELECT COUNT(*) AS CarCount FROM cleaned_cars_data WHERE Accelerate > (SELECT MaxHorsepower FROM max_horsepower)

question: What is the number of cars with a greater accelerate than the one with the most horsepower?
basic_sql: WITH max_horsepower AS (SELECT MAX(Horsepower) AS MaxHorsepower FROM cars_data) SELECT COUNT(*) AS CarCount FROM cars_data WHERE CAST(REPLACE(accelerate, ',', '') AS REAL) > (SELECT MaxHorsepower FROM max_horsepower)
robust_sql: WITH max_horsepower AS (SELECT MAX(CAST(REPLACE(Horsepower, ',', '') AS REAL)) AS MaxHorsepower FROM cars_data), cleaned_cars_data AS (SELECT Id, CAST(REPLACE(accelerate, ',', '') AS REAL) AS Accelerate FROM cars_data WHERE NOT Horsepower IS NULL AND NOT Accelerate IS NULL) SELECT COUNT(*) AS CarCount FROM cleaned_cars_data WHERE Accelerate > (SELECT MaxHorsepower FROM max_horsepower)

question: Which model has the most version(make) of cars?
basic_sql: WITH model_versions AS (SELECT ml.Model, cn.Make, COUNT(cn.MakeId) AS VersionCount FROM model_list AS ml JOIN car_names AS cn ON ml.ModelId = cn.ModelId GROUP BY ml.Model, cn.Make) SELECT Model, MAX(VersionCount) AS MostVersions FROM model_versions
robust_sql: WITH model_versions AS (SELECT ml.Model, cn.Make, COUNT(cn.MakeId) AS VersionCount FROM model_list AS ml JOIN car_names AS cn ON ml.ModelId = cn.ModelId GROUP BY ml.Model, cn.Make), dedup_model_versions AS (SELECT DISTINCT Model, Make FROM model_versions), final_result AS (SELECT mv.Model, mv.VersionCount FROM model_versions AS mv JOIN dedup_model_versions AS dmv ON mv.Model = dmv.Model AND mv.Make = dmv.Make ORDER BY VersionCount DESC LIMIT 1) SELECT * FROM final_result

question: What model has the most different versions?
basic_sql: WITH model_versions AS (SELECT m.Model, COUNT(DISTINCT cn.Make) AS VersionCount FROM model_list AS m JOIN car_names AS cn ON m.ModelId = cn.MakeId GROUP BY m.Model) SELECT Model, VersionCount FROM model_versions ORDER BY VersionCount DESC LIMIT 1

question: What is the date and id of the transcript with the least number of results?
basic_sql: WITH DedupedTranscripts AS (SELECT DISTINCT transcript_id, transcript_date FROM Transcripts), CountedTranscripts AS (SELECT transcript_id, COUNT(*) AS result_count FROM DedupedTranscripts JOIN Transcript_Contents ON DedupedTranscripts.transcript_id = Transcript_Contents.transcript_id GROUP BY transcript_id) SELECT transcript_id, transcript_date FROM CountedTranscripts ORDER BY result_count ASC LIMIT 1

question: Who are enrolled in 2 degree programs in one semester? List the first name, middle name and last name and the id.
basic_sql: WITH StudentEnrollments AS (SELECT se.student_id, se.degree_program_id, se.semester_id FROM Student_Enrolment AS se GROUP BY se.student_id, se.degree_program_id, se.semester_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT se.degree_program_id) = 2 AND COUNT(DISTINCT se.semester_id) = 1), SelectedStudents AS (SELECT s.student_id, s.first_name, s.middle_name, s.last_name FROM Students AS s JOIN StudentEnrollments AS se ON s.student_id = se.student_id) SELECT student_id, first_name, middle_name, last_name FROM SelectedStudents
robust_sql: WITH StudentEnrollments AS (SELECT se.student_id, se.degree_program_id, se.semester_id FROM Student_Enrolment AS se GROUP BY se.student_id, se.degree_program_id, se.semester_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT se.degree_program_id) = 2 AND COUNT(DISTINCT se.semester_id) = 1), SelectedStudents AS (SELECT s.student_id, COALESCE(s.first_name, 'Unknown') AS first_name, COALESCE(s.middle_name, 'Unknown') AS middle_name, COALESCE(s.last_name, 'Unknown') AS last_name FROM Students AS s JOIN StudentEnrollments AS se ON s.student_id = se.student_id) SELECT student_id, first_name, middle_name, last_name FROM SelectedStudents

question: What is the semester which most student registered in? Show both the name and the id.
basic_sql: WITH StudentEnrolmentCounts AS (SELECT s.semester_id, COUNT(DISTINCT se.student_id) AS student_count FROM Students AS s JOIN Student_Enrolment AS se ON s.student_id = se.student_id GROUP BY s.semester_id) SELECT sc.semester_id, sc.semester_name FROM StudentEnrolmentCounts AS sec JOIN Semesters AS sc ON sec.semester_id = sc.semester_id ORDER BY sec.student_count DESC LIMIT 1
robust_sql: WITH StudentEnrolmentCounts AS (SELECT s.semester_id, COUNT(DISTINCT se.student_id) AS student_count FROM Students AS s JOIN Student_Enrolment AS se ON s.student_id = se.student_id GROUP BY s.semester_id), DeduplicatedStudents AS (SELECT DISTINCT student_id FROM Students), DeduplicatedStudentEnrolment AS (SELECT DISTINCT student_id, semester_id FROM Student_Enrolment), DeduplicatedSemesters AS (SELECT DISTINCT semester_id, semester_name FROM Semesters) SELECT sc.semester_id, sc.semester_name FROM StudentEnrolmentCounts AS sec JOIN DeduplicatedSemesters AS sc ON sec.semester_id = sc.semester_id ORDER BY sec.student_count DESC LIMIT 1

question: Show the date of the transcript which shows the least number of results, also list the id.
basic_sql: WITH DedupedTranscripts AS (SELECT DISTINCT transcript_id, transcript_date FROM Transcripts), CountedTranscripts AS (SELECT transcript_id, COUNT(*) AS result_count FROM Transcript_Contents GROUP BY transcript_id) SELECT dt.transcript_id, dt.transcript_date FROM DedupedTranscripts AS dt JOIN CountedTranscripts AS ct ON dt.transcript_id = ct.transcript_id ORDER BY ct.result_count ASC LIMIT 1
robust_sql: WITH DedupedTranscripts AS (SELECT DISTINCT transcript_id, transcript_date FROM Transcripts), CountedTranscripts AS (SELECT transcript_id, COUNT(*) AS result_count FROM Transcript_Contents GROUP BY transcript_id) SELECT dt.transcript_id, dt.transcript_date FROM DedupedTranscripts AS dt JOIN CountedTranscripts AS ct ON dt.transcript_id = ct.transcript_id ORDER BY ct.result_count ASC LIMIT 1

question: What is the first, middle, and last name, along with the id and number of enrollments, for the student who enrolled the most in any program?
basic_sql: SELECT s.first_name, s.middle_name, s.last_name, s.student_id, COUNT(se.student_enrolment_id) AS enrollment_count FROM Students AS s JOIN Student_Enrolment AS se ON s.student_id = se.student_id GROUP BY s.student_id, s.first_name, s.middle_name, s.last_name ORDER BY enrollment_count DESC LIMIT 1
robust_sql: WITH Cleaned_Students AS (SELECT DISTINCT student_id, first_name, middle_name, last_name FROM Students), Cleaned_Student_Enrolment AS (SELECT DISTINCT student_enrolment_id, student_id FROM Student_Enrolment) SELECT cs.first_name, cs.middle_name, cs.last_name, cs.student_id, COUNT(cse.student_enrolment_id) AS enrollment_count FROM Cleaned_Students AS cs JOIN Cleaned_Student_Enrolment AS cse ON cs.student_id = cse.student_id GROUP BY cs.student_id, cs.first_name, cs.middle_name, cs.last_name ORDER BY enrollment_count DESC LIMIT 1

question: Which student has enrolled for the most times in any program? List the id, first name, middle name, last name, the number of enrollments and student id.
basic_sql: SELECT s.student_id, s.first_name, s.middle_name, s.last_name, COUNT(se.student_enrolment_id) AS enrollment_count FROM Students AS s JOIN Student_Enrolment AS se ON s.student_id = se.student_id GROUP BY s.student_id, s.first_name, s.middle_name, s.last_name ORDER BY enrollment_count DESC LIMIT 1
robust_sql: WITH StudentEnrolments AS (SELECT se.student_id, s.first_name, s.middle_name, s.last_name, COUNT(se.student_enrolment_id) AS enrollment_count FROM Students AS s JOIN Student_Enrolment AS se ON s.student_id = se.student_id GROUP BY s.student_id, s.first_name, s.middle_name, s.last_name), DeduplicatedStudents AS (SELECT DISTINCT student_id, first_name, middle_name, last_name FROM StudentEnrolments) SELECT student_id, first_name, middle_name, last_name, enrollment_count FROM StudentEnrolments WHERE (student_id, first_name, middle_name, last_name) IN (SELECT * FROM DeduplicatedStudents) ORDER BY enrollment_count DESC LIMIT 1

question: For each semester, what is the name and id of the one with the most students registered?
basic_sql: WITH Enrolled_Semesters AS (SELECT s.semester_id, s.semester_name, COUNT(se.student_id) AS student_count FROM Students AS st JOIN Student_Enrolment AS se ON st.student_id = se.student_id JOIN Semesters AS s ON se.semester_id = s.semester_id GROUP BY s.semester_id, s.semester_name), Ranked_Semesters AS (SELECT semester_id, semester_name, student_count, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY student_count DESC) AS rank FROM Enrolled_Semesters) SELECT semester_name, semester_id FROM Ranked_Semesters WHERE rank = 1
robust_sql: WITH Enrolled_Semesters AS (SELECT s.semester_id, s.semester_name, COUNT(se.student_id) AS student_count FROM Students AS st JOIN Student_Enrolment AS se ON st.student_id = se.student_id JOIN Semesters AS s ON se.semester_id = s.semester_id GROUP BY s.semester_id, s.semester_name), Ranked_Semesters AS (SELECT semester_id, semester_name, student_count, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY student_count DESC) AS rank FROM Enrolled_Semesters) SELECT semester_name, semester_id FROM Ranked_Semesters WHERE rank = 1
